understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to solve each level by positioning all selectable pieces onto the goal tiles marked with '+' at the same time; once every goal tile was occupied simultaneously, the level would report WIN, and completing all levels would win the game.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Actions were issued as grid-based moves (MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN) or CLICKs at specific [x,y] coordinates. CLICKing either selected a piece or attempted to move the currently selected piece to the clicked target square depending on the level’s rules; moves failed when the destination was blocked by walls/obstacles or an invalid tile. Piece positions updated only when a move was legal; repeated CLICKs on blocked targets produced no movement. With multiple pieces, selection switching was done by CLICKing on the other piece’s position, and success required coordinating all pieces so they simultaneously occupied the '+' goal squares.

Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to solve each level by positioning all selectable pieces onto the goal tiles marked with '+' at the same time; once every goal tile was occupied simultaneously, the level would report WIN, and completing all levels would win the game.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Actions were issued as grid-based moves (MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN) or CLICKs at specific [x,y] coordinates. CLICKing either selected a piece or attempted to move the currently selected piece to the clicked target square depending on the level’s rules; moves failed when the destination was blocked by walls/obstacles or an invalid tile. Piece positions updated only when a move was legal; repeated CLICKs on blocked targets produced no movement. With multiple pieces, selection switching was done by CLICKing on the other piece’s position, and success required coordinating all pieces so they simultaneously occupied the '+' goal squares.
